presentation_variants.presenting_complaint: Pressure in my chest when I exert myself
Chest pain that goes away when I rest
Tightness in my chest while climbing stairs

history_data.hpi: Patient reports intermittent central chest pressure for the past several months. Symptoms are triggered by physical exertion such as climbing stairs or brisk walking and consistently improve within a few minutes of rest. No symptoms occur at rest.
patient_quotes: It usually happens when I'm active.
The pain goes away when I stop and rest.
Walking uphill almost always brings it on.
I've noticed it getting slightly more frequent.
I feel completely fine when resting.
pmh_pool: Family history of coronary artery disease

abnormal_investigations.ECG: Normal sinus rhythm. Non-specific ST-T changes in lateral leads. No acute ischaemia. No Q waves.
abnormal_investigations.Troponin: 0.01 ng/mL (within normal limits — no evidence of myocardial necrosis; consistent with stable rather than acute coronary syndrome)
abnormal_investigations.Chest X-Ray: No acute cardiopulmonary abnormality. Normal cardiac silhouette. No pulmonary oedema.
abnormal_investigations.Lipid Profile: Total cholesterol 6.2 mmol/L (elevated). LDL 4.1 mmol/L (elevated). HDL 0.9 mmol/L (low). Triglycerides 2.4 mmol/L (elevated).
abnormal_investigations.HbA1c: 58 mmol/mol (7.5%) — suboptimal glycaemic control in setting of known type 2 diabetes
abnormal_investigations.Fasting Blood Glucose: 8.1 mmol/L (elevated — consistent with known type 2 diabetes)
abnormal_investigations.Urea & Electrolytes: Na 138 mmol/L, K 4.1 mmol/L, Creatinine 91 µmol/L — normal
abnormal_investigations.LFTs (Liver Function Tests): Within normal limits — important prior to statin initiation or dose escalation
abnormal_investigations.Exercise Stress Test: ST depression of 1.5mm in leads V4–V6 at 75% maximum predicted heart rate. Symptoms reproduced. Test terminated at Stage 3 Bruce protocol. Positive for inducible ischaemia.
abnormal_investigations.CT Coronary Angiography: Moderate stenosis of the left anterior descending artery (60–70%). Mild disease in right coronary artery. No critical lesions identified.

critical_actions: arrange stress testing or CT coronary angiography
red_flags: Chest pain consistently relieved by rest
management_actions: Arrange stress testing or CT coronary angiography
